signals.headline: Fed Maintains Rates Unchanged, Projects Single 2026 Cut** | YES | HIGH confidence
signals.raw:
Fed Maintains Rates Unchanged, Projects Single 2026 Cut** | YES | HIGH confidence

The Federal Reserve held interest rates steady between 3.5% and 3.75%, with Chair Jerome Powell confirming the Fed projects only a single rate reduction for all of 2026[1]. Powell emphasized that progress on inflation—particularly through reduction in goods inflation as tariff effects normalize—is critical to any fu
matched_markets: Will Powell say "Tariff inflation" during April press conference?

signals.headline: Core Inflation Remains Elevated at ~3%, with Tariffs as Major Driver** | YES | HIGH confidence
signals.raw:
Core Inflation Remains Elevated at ~3%, with Tariffs as Major Driver** | YES | HIGH confidence

Core inflation sits around 3%, with tariffs accounting for between 0.5 and 0.75 percentage points of this figure[1]. The Fed is specifically watching for tariff inflation to decline mid-year as one-time price effects work through the economy[1].
matched_markets: Will Powell say "Tariff inflation" during April press conference?

signals.headline: Oil Price Surge from Iran Conflict Pushes Overall Inflation to 3
signals.raw:
Oil Price Surge from Iran Conflict Pushes Overall Inflation to 3.3%** | YES | HIGH confidence

Recent CPI data showed prices rose 3.3% over 12 months, driven significantly by rising gas prices stemming from the Iran-U.S. conflict and disruptions to Strait of Hormuz oil supplies[2]. This geopolitical factor is now a key inflation variable affecting Fed decision-making[2].
matched_markets: Strait of Hormuz traffic returns to normal by end of April?

signals.headline: Market Pricing: 97% Probability of April 28 Hold** | YES | HIGH confidence
signals.raw:
Market Pricing: 97% Probability of April 28 Hold** | YES | HIGH confidence

Traders are pricing a more than 97% probability that the Fed will maintain rates at its April 28 meeting, with only 2.6% pricing in a potential hike[2].
